content:
# Buy a Cheap Subaru in the UK: Your Guide to Reliability, Issues & Tips

Fancy getting your hands on a Subaru without breaking the bank? You're in the right place! Subarus have a legendary reputation for their go-anywhere Symmetrical All-Wheel Drive and robust Boxer engines. But can you really find a **cheap Subaru UK** that’s still a cracking deal? Absolutely, if you know what to look for.

Here at BuyAndScrap, we believe everyone deserves a reliable car, whatever their budget. This guide will walk you through what makes Subarus great, which models offer the best value, common issues to keep an eye on, and crucial tips for bagging yourself a fantastic **cheap Subaru in the UK**.

## Why Choose a Subaru? The All-Wheel Drive Advantage

Subarus aren't like other cars. Their defining features are the horizontally opposed 'Boxer' engine, which offers a lower centre of gravity for better handling, and their renowned Symmetrical All-Wheel Drive system. This isn't just for off-road enthusiasts; it provides incredible grip and stability in all sorts of British weather conditions, from greasy country lanes to snowy commutes. If you value safety, reliability, and capability, a Subaru could be your perfect match – even on a budget.

## Finding a Cheap Subaru in the UK: Popular Models

When you're searching for a **cheap Subaru UK**, certain models tend to pop up more often and offer fantastic value for money.

### Subaru Impreza

Beyond the iconic WRX and STI, the standard Impreza hatchback or saloon is a solid, often overlooked choice. They're practical, reliable, and still benefit from that excellent AWD system. You can often find well-maintained examples for surprisingly little, making them a brilliant entry point into Subaru ownership.

### Subaru Forester

If you need something a bit more substantial, the Forester SUV is a perennial favourite. It offers more ground clearance, a spacious interior, and fantastic practicality for families or those who enjoy outdoor pursuits. Early generations, especially, can be very affordable and tough as old boots.

### Subaru Outback

Essentially a raised Legacy estate, the Outback combines the comfort of a saloon with the versatility and soft-roading capability of an SUV. They're great for long journeys, have huge boot space, and are incredibly comfortable. Older Outbacks often fly under the radar, presenting a fantastic opportunity for a bargain.

### Subaru Legacy

Perhaps the unsung hero of the Subaru lineup. The Legacy saloon and estate are often forgotten in favour of the Impreza or Forester but offer excellent value. They're refined, comfortable, and reliable, and still come with the peace of mind of AWD. If you're after a sensible, well-built car, a cheap Legacy is hard to beat.

## Subaru Reliability: What to Expect When Buying Cheap

Generally, Subarus are known for their strong build quality and reliability, often outlasting many competitors. However, when you're looking for a cheaper, older model, it's crucial to understand that age and mileage will inevitably bring wear and tear. A well-serviced Subaru can easily rack up huge mileage, but neglected ones can be a money pit. Always prioritise a car with a comprehensive service history.

## Common Issues to Look Out For (Especially on Older, Cheaper Models)

No car is perfect, and older Subarus have a few well-known quirks you should be aware of before buying a **cheap Subaru in the UK**.

### Head Gaskets (Older 2.5L Engines)

This is perhaps the most famous Subaru issue. Older 2.5-litre non-turbo Boxer engines (found in some Outbacks, Foresters, and Legacys from the late 90s to mid-2000s) are prone to head gasket failure. Symptoms include oil leaks, coolant consumption, or white smoke from the exhaust. Check for a history of head gasket replacement or, if buying a car with one, factor in potential repair costs.

### Rust

Like many cars exposed to British weather, rust can be an issue. Pay close attention to the rear wheel arches, sills, and subframes. Get underneath and have a good look if you can.

### Suspension Components

Given their AWD capability and often enthusiastic owners, suspension components like bushes, anti-roll bar links, and shock absorbers can wear out. Listen for knocks and creaks during the test drive.

### Electrical Gremlins

Less common than mechanical issues, but check all electrics – windows, central locking, air conditioning, and dashboard lights – are working as they should.

### Clutch and Gearbox

On manual cars, particularly those that have seen hard use (like older WRX/STI models, though less likely on your average cheap Subaru), check the clutch operation and ensure gear changes are smooth without crunching or difficulty engaging gears.

## Top Tips for Buying a Cheap Subaru in the UK

Finding a bargain doesn't mean compromising on due diligence. Here's how to ensure you're getting a good deal:

### Check the Service History (FSH is Gold!)

This is paramount. A full-service history (FSH) shows the car has been looked after. Look for evidence of regular oil changes and major service items like timing belts being addressed. If you're also looking to offload your current motor, check out how easy it is to [sell your car](/sell-your-car) through us.

### Get an Independent Inspection

For peace of mind, especially if you're not mechanically minded, consider an independent inspection. It can flag up costly issues before you buy.

### Test Drive Thoroughly

Don't rush it! Drive at various speeds, listen for unusual noises, check the brakes, and ensure the AWD system feels right (if you can find a safe, quiet patch of gravel or grass, feel for the traction).

### Be Realistic

A cheap Subaru will have some miles on the clock and likely a few battle scars. Don't expect perfection, but do ensure any issues are reflected in the price and are manageable.

### Factor in Maintenance Costs

Even a reliable car needs maintenance. Budget for servicing, tyres, and potentially a repair or two, especially with an older vehicle.

## Ready to Find Your Subaru?

Finding a **cheap Subaru UK** is entirely achievable with a bit of research and knowing what to look for. These cars offer exceptional value, especially their legendary AWD system, making them a smart choice for many British drivers. For more general advice on picking up a pre-loved vehicle, our [used car buying guide](/used-car-buying-guide) is packed with useful info.
